creator: rea@FreeBSD.org
text:
Tue, Dec 21, 2010 at 06:45:30PM +0300, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
 > Sat, Dec 18, 2010 at 11:05:21PM +0100, olli hauer wrote:
 > >  - move to new patch format which can be maintained
 > >    with command 'make makepatch', this makes it easier
 > >    for everyone to write patches.
 [...]
 > 
 > I am against this change.  'make makepatch' is a poor's man approach: it
 > loses diff hunk grouping and commit metadata.  I had just wrote a
 > message to freebsd-ports@ you're in Cc, so I think that this should be
 > at least discussed.
 
 There were no answers to my post to the freebsd-ports@, but my
 mentors (garga@ and erwin@) told me that maintainers can use
 whatever format that suits one.  And at this stage I am inclined
 to use commit-oriented approach, sorry for that.
 
 So, given that the port was updated and most of your changes
 were incorporated into that patch, do you have some corrections
 or additions or I can just close this PR?
 
 Thanks!
 -- 
 Eygene Ryabinkin                                        ,,,^..^,,,
 [ Life's unfair - but root password helps!           | codelabs.ru ]
